# Soroban Smart Contract Development Guide
# SDK Version: 21.0.0

This document contains essential patterns and API documentation for writing Soroban smart contracts on the Stellar blockchain.

## COMPLETE SDK 21.0.0 API REFERENCE

### Env Methods (always available as first parameter)
```rust
env.storage()                       // Access storage
env.events()                        // Access events
env.ledger()                        // Access ledger info
env.current_contract_address()      // Get this contract's Address
env.deployer()                      // Access deployer functionality
env.crypto()                        // Access crypto functions
env.prng()                          // Access random number generator
```

### Storage API
```rust
// Instance storage (contract-level, persists with contract)
env.storage().instance().set(&key, &value)           // Write value
env.storage().instance().get(&key) -> Option<T>      // Read value (returns Option)
env.storage().instance().has(&key) -> bool           // Check exists
env.storage().instance().remove(&key)                // Delete key
env.storage().instance().extend_ttl(threshold, extend_to)  // Extend TTL

// Persistent storage (user data, longer TTL)
env.storage().persistent().set(&key, &value)
env.storage().persistent().get(&key) -> Option<T>
env.storage().persistent().has(&key) -> bool
env.storage().persistent().remove(&key)

// Temporary storage (short-lived)
env.storage().temporary().set(&key, &value)
env.storage().temporary().get(&key) -> Option<T>
```

### Address Methods
```rust
address.require_auth()              // Verify caller authorized this call (REQUIRED before state changes!)
address.require_auth_for_args(args) // Verify auth with specific args
address == other_address            // Compare addresses
address.clone()                     // Clone the address
```

### Vec<T> Methods (IMPORTANT: use push_back, NOT push!)
```rust
Vec::new(&env) -> Vec<T>            // Create empty vec
vec.push_back(item)                 // Add to end (NOT .push()!)
vec.push_front(item)                // Add to start
vec.pop_back() -> Option<T>         // Remove from end
vec.pop_front() -> Option<T>        // Remove from start
vec.get(index) -> Option<T>         // Get by index
vec.set(index, value)               // Set by index
vec.remove(index) -> Option<T>      // Remove by index
vec.len() -> u32                    // Get length
vec.is_empty() -> bool              // Check if empty
vec.first() -> Option<T>            // Get first
vec.last() -> Option<T>             // Get last
vec.iter() -> Iterator              // Iterate (requires T: Clone!)
vec.contains(&item) -> bool         // Check contains
vec.append(&other_vec)              // Append another vec
```

### Map<K, V> Methods (IMPORTANT: takes OWNED keys, not &references!)
```rust
Map::new(&env) -> Map<K, V>         // Create empty map
map.set(key, value)                 // Set (OWNED key, not &key!)
map.get(key) -> Option<V>           // Get (OWNED key, not &key!)
map.contains_key(key) -> bool       // Check key exists (OWNED key!)
map.remove(key) -> Option<V>        // Remove (OWNED key!)
map.len() -> u32                    // Get size
map.is_empty() -> bool              // Check if empty
map.keys() -> Vec<K>                // Get all keys
map.values() -> Vec<V>              // Get all values
```

### String Methods
```rust
String::from_str(&env, "text")      // Create from &str (NOT String::from!)
string.len() -> u32                 // Get length
```

### Symbol Methods  
```rust
Symbol::new(&env, "name")           // Create symbol (NOT Symbol::from!)
symbol_short!("short")              // Create short symbol (<= 9 chars)
```

### token::Client Methods (for Stellar assets)
```rust
token::Client::new(&env, &token_address)  // Create client
client.transfer(&from, &to, &amount)      // Transfer tokens
client.balance(&address) -> i128          // Get balance
client.decimals() -> u32                  // Get decimals
client.name() -> String                   // Get name
client.symbol() -> Symbol                 // Get symbol
client.allowance(&from, &spender) -> i128 // Get allowance
client.approve(&from, &spender, &amount, &expiration)  // Approve spending
client.burn(&from, &amount)               // Burn tokens
client.mint(&to, &amount)                 // Mint tokens (admin only)
```

### Ledger Info
```rust
env.ledger().timestamp() -> u64     // Current timestamp (seconds)
env.ledger().sequence() -> u32      // Current ledger sequence
env.ledger().network_id() -> Bytes  // Network ID
```

### Events
```rust
env.events().publish(
    (topic1, topic2, ...),           // Topics tuple
    (data1, data2, ...)              // Data tuple
)
```

### Crypto Functions
```rust
env.crypto().sha256(&bytes) -> BytesN<32>
env.crypto().keccak256(&bytes) -> BytesN<32>
env.crypto().ed25519_verify(&public_key, &message, &signature) -> bool
```

### Random Numbers (PRNG) — IMPORTANT: use ONLY these methods!
```rust
env.prng().gen::<u64>()                   // Generate random u64
env.prng().gen_range::<u64>(0..100)       // Random u64 in [0, 100)
env.prng().gen_range::<u64>(1..=6)        // Random u64 in [1, 6] (dice roll)
env.prng().gen_range::<u64>(0..2)         // Coin flip: 0 or 1
env.prng().u64_in_range(0..=1)            // Also coin flip (u64 helper)
env.prng().gen_len::<Bytes>(32)           // Random Bytes of length 32
env.prng().fill(&mut value)               // Fill type with random data
env.prng().shuffle(&mut vec)              // Shuffle a Vec
env.prng().seed(bytes)                    // Reseed the PRNG
```
⚠️ FORBIDDEN methods (DO NOT EXIST):
- `env.prng().random_bytes()` — DOES NOT EXIST
- `env.prng().random()` — DOES NOT EXIST
- `env.prng().next()` — DOES NOT EXIST
- `env.prng().rand()` — DOES NOT EXIST

## ⚠️ CRITICAL MISTAKES TO AVOID (SDK 21)

### MISTAKE 1: Map takes OWNED keys, NOT references!
```rust
// WRONG - ERROR: expected Address, found &Address
balances.get(&user)
map.contains_key(&key)

// CORRECT - use .clone()
balances.get(user.clone())
map.contains_key(key.clone())
```

### MISTAKE 2: Vec uses push_back(), NOT push()!
```rust
// WRONG - no method named `push`
items.push(value);

// CORRECT
items.push_back(value);
```

### MISTAKE 3: Custom structs in Vec need #[derive(Clone)]
```rust
// CORRECT
#[contracttype]
#[derive(Clone)]  // REQUIRED!

pub struct MyStruct { ... }
```

### MISTAKE 4: No to_string() in no_std!
```rust
// WRONG
let id = num.to_string();

// CORRECT - use integers directly
let id: u64 = counter;
```

### MISTAKE 5: Use Symbol::new() and String::from_str()
```rust
// WRONG - doesn't exist!
Symbol::from("name")
String::from("text")

// CORRECT
Symbol::new(&env, "name")
String::from_str(&env, "text")
```

## Contract Structure

Every Soroban contract must follow this structure:

```rust
#![no_std]
use soroban_sdk::{contract, contractimpl, Env};

#[contract]
pub struct MyContract;

#[contractimpl]
impl MyContract {
    pub fn my_function(env: Env) {
        // implementation
    }
}
```

## Core Imports from soroban_sdk

```rust
// Always required
use soroban_sdk::{contract, contractimpl, Env};

// Types - import only what you use
use soroban_sdk::{
    Address,      // Wallet/contract addresses
    String,       // String type (not std::String)
    Symbol,       // Symbol identifiers  
    Vec,          // Vector type
    Map,          // Key-value map
    Bytes,        // Raw bytes
    BytesN,       // Fixed-size bytes
};

// Macros and attributes
use soroban_sdk::{
    contracttype,    // For custom types/enums
    contracterror,   // For custom errors
    symbol_short,    // For short symbols (<= 9 chars)
    log,            // For debug logging
};

// Token operations
use soroban_sdk::token;
```

## CRITICAL: Symbol Creation (SDK 21.0.0)

```rust
// CORRECT - Always use Symbol::new with env reference
let sym = Symbol::new(&env, "my_symbol_name");

// For short symbols (9 chars or less), you can use the macro
let short_sym = symbol_short!("short");

// WRONG - These do NOT exist in SDK 21
// Symbol::from("name")  // COMPILATION ERROR
// "name".into()         // COMPILATION ERROR
```

## CRITICAL: String Creation (SDK 21.0.0)

```rust
// CORRECT - Always use String::from_str with env reference
let s = String::from_str(&env, "hello world");

// WRONG - These do NOT exist in SDK 21
// String::from("hello")  // COMPILATION ERROR
// "hello".into()         // COMPILATION ERROR
```

## Storage API

Soroban provides three storage types:

### Instance Storage (contract-level, persists with contract)
```rust
// Write
env.storage().instance().set(&key, &value);

// Read with default
let val: i128 = env.storage().instance().get(&key).unwrap_or(0);

// Read required (panics if missing)
let val: Address = env.storage().instance().get(&key).unwrap();

// Check existence
let exists: bool = env.storage().instance().has(&key);

// Remove
env.storage().instance().remove(&key);
```

### Persistent Storage (user data, longer TTL)
```rust
env.storage().persistent().set(&key, &value);
let val: i128 = env.storage().persistent().get(&key).unwrap_or(0);
```

### Temporary Storage (ephemeral, short TTL)
```rust
env.storage().temporary().set(&key, &value);
let val: i128 = env.storage().temporary().get(&key).unwrap_or(0);
```

## Storage Keys with contracttype

```rust
#[contracttype]
#[derive(Clone)]
pub enum DataKey {
    Admin,                    // Simple key
    Counter,                  // Simple key
    Balance(Address),         // Parameterized key
    Allowance(Address, Address), // Multi-param key
}

// Usage
env.storage().instance().set(&DataKey::Admin, &admin_address);
env.storage().persistent().set(&DataKey::Balance(user.clone()), &amount);
```

## Authentication with require_auth

```rust
// EVERY function that modifies state MUST verify authorization
pub fn transfer(env: Env, from: Address, to: Address, amount: i128) {
    // Verify 'from' has authorized this call
    from.require_auth();
    
    // Now safe to proceed with state changes
}

// For admin-only functions
pub fn admin_action(env: Env, caller: Address) {
    caller.require_auth();
    
    let admin: Address = env.storage().instance().get(&DataKey::Admin).unwrap();
    if caller != admin {
        panic!("unauthorized");
    }
}
```

## Token Operations

```rust
use soroban_sdk::token;

// Create token client
let token_client = token::Client::new(&env, &token_address);

// Transfer tokens (from must have called require_auth)
token_client.transfer(&from, &to, &amount);

// Get balance (read-only, no auth needed)
let balance: i128 = token_client.balance(&address);

// Get contract's own address for receiving tokens
let contract_addr = env.current_contract_address();
```

## Custom Errors

```rust
#[contracterror]
#[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, PartialOrd, Ord)]
#[repr(u32)]
pub enum ContractError {
    NotInitialized = 1,
    AlreadyInitialized = 2,
    Unauthorized = 3,
    InsufficientBalance = 4,
    InvalidAmount = 5,
}

// Usage - return Result or panic
pub fn withdraw(env: Env, amount: i128) -> Result<(), ContractError> {
    if amount <= 0 {
        return Err(ContractError::InvalidAmount);
    }
    Ok(())
}

// Or simply panic with message
pub fn withdraw(env: Env, amount: i128) {
    if amount <= 0 {
        panic!("invalid amount");
    }
}
```

## Events

```rust
// Publish events for important state changes
env.events().publish(
    (Symbol::new(&env, "transfer"),),  // Topics (tuple)
    (from.clone(), to.clone(), amount) // Data (tuple)
);

// Multiple topics
env.events().publish(
    (Symbol::new(&env, "swap"), Symbol::new(&env, "executed")),
    (user, amount_in, amount_out)
);
```

## Vec (Vector) Operations

⚠️ IMPORTANT: Vec uses push_back(), NOT push()! Custom types need #[derive(Clone)] for iteration!

```rust
use soroban_sdk::Vec;

// Create empty vec
let mut items: Vec<Address> = Vec::new(&env);

// Add items - USE push_back NOT push!
items.push_back(addr1);
items.push_back(addr2);

// Get item
let first: Address = items.get(0).unwrap();

// Get length
let len: u32 = items.len();

// Iterate (requires T: Clone for custom types!)
for item in items.iter() {
    // use item
}

// For custom types in Vec, you MUST derive Clone:
#[contracttype]
#[derive(Clone)]  // REQUIRED for .iter() to work!
pub struct MyItem {
    pub value: i128,
}
```

## Map Operations

⚠️ IMPORTANT: Map methods take OWNED values, NOT references! Use .clone() when needed!

```rust
use soroban_sdk::Map;

// Create empty map
let mut balances: Map<Address, i128> = Map::new(&env);

// Set value - OWNED value, not reference!
balances.set(user.clone(), 100i128);

// Get value - OWNED key, not &key!
let bal: i128 = balances.get(user.clone()).unwrap_or(0);

// Check existence - OWNED key, not &key!
let exists: bool = balances.contains_key(user.clone());

// Remove - OWNED key!
balances.remove(user.clone());

// WRONG - These will NOT compile:
// balances.get(&user)         // ERROR!
// balances.contains_key(&key) // ERROR!
```

## Complete Contract Examples

### Example 1: Simple Counter
```rust
#![no_std]
use soroban_sdk::{contract, contractimpl, contracttype, Address, Env, Symbol};

#[contracttype]
pub enum DataKey {
    Count,
    Admin,
}

#[contract]
pub struct Counter;

#[contractimpl]
impl Counter {
    pub fn init(env: Env, admin: Address) {
        env.storage().instance().set(&DataKey::Admin, &admin);
        env.storage().instance().set(&DataKey::Count, &0u64);
    }

    pub fn increment(env: Env, user: Address) -> u64 {
        user.require_auth();
        let mut count: u64 = env.storage().instance().get(&DataKey::Count).unwrap_or(0);
        count += 1;
        env.storage().instance().set(&DataKey::Count, &count);
        env.events().publish((Symbol::new(&env, "increment"),), count);
        count
    }

    pub fn get(env: Env) -> u64 {
        env.storage().instance().get(&DataKey::Count).unwrap_or(0)
    }
}
```

### Example 2: Token Vault
```rust
#![no_std]
use soroban_sdk::{contract, contractimpl, contracttype, token, Address, Env, Symbol};

#[contracttype]
pub enum DataKey {
    Token,
    Deposit(Address),
}

#[contract]
pub struct Vault;

#[contractimpl]
impl Vault {
    pub fn init(env: Env, token: Address) {
        env.storage().instance().set(&DataKey::Token, &token);
    }

    pub fn deposit(env: Env, from: Address, amount: i128) {
        from.require_auth();
        
        let token_addr: Address = env.storage().instance().get(&DataKey::Token).unwrap();
        let client = token::Client::new(&env, &token_addr);
        client.transfer(&from, &env.current_contract_address(), &amount);
        
        let key = DataKey::Deposit(from.clone());
        let current: i128 = env.storage().persistent().get(&key).unwrap_or(0);
        env.storage().persistent().set(&key, &(current + amount));
        
        env.events().publish((Symbol::new(&env, "deposit"),), (from, amount));
    }

    pub fn withdraw(env: Env, to: Address, amount: i128) {
        to.require_auth();
        
        let key = DataKey::Deposit(to.clone());
        let balance: i128 = env.storage().persistent().get(&key).unwrap_or(0);
        if balance < amount {
            panic!("insufficient balance");
        }
        
        let token_addr: Address = env.storage().instance().get(&DataKey::Token).unwrap();
        let client = token::Client::new(&env, &token_addr);
        client.transfer(&env.current_contract_address(), &to, &amount);
        
        env.storage().persistent().set(&key, &(balance - amount));
        env.events().publish((Symbol::new(&env, "withdraw"),), (to, amount));
    }

    pub fn balance(env: Env, user: Address) -> i128 {
        env.storage().persistent().get(&DataKey::Deposit(user)).unwrap_or(0)
    }
}
```

### Example 3: Fund Transfer
```rust
#![no_std]
use soroban_sdk::{contract, contractimpl, contracttype, token, Address, Env, Symbol, String};

#[contracttype]
#[derive(Clone)]
pub enum DataKey {
    Admin,
    TokenAddress,
    TransferCount,
}

#[contract]
pub struct FundTransfer;

#[contractimpl]
impl FundTransfer {
    /// Initialize the contract
    pub fn initialize(env: Env, admin: Address, token_address: Address) {
        if env.storage().instance().has(&DataKey::Admin) {
            panic!("already initialized");
        }
        env.storage().instance().set(&DataKey::Admin, &admin);
        env.storage().instance().set(&DataKey::TokenAddress, &token_address);
        env.storage().instance().set(&DataKey::TransferCount, &0u64);
    }

    /// Transfer funds from sender to recipient
    pub fn transfer(env: Env, from: Address, to: Address, amount: i128) -> u64 {
        from.require_auth();
        
        if amount <= 0 {
            panic!("amount must be positive");
        }
        
        let token_addr: Address = env.storage().instance().get(&DataKey::TokenAddress).unwrap();
        let token = token::Client::new(&env, &token_addr);
        
        // Execute transfer
        token.transfer(&from, &to, &amount);
        
        // Increment transfer count
        let mut count: u64 = env.storage().instance().get(&DataKey::TransferCount).unwrap_or(0);
        count += 1;
        env.storage().instance().set(&DataKey::TransferCount, &count);
        
        // Emit event
        env.events().publish(
            (Symbol::new(&env, "transfer"),),
            (from, to, amount, count)
        );
        
        count
    }

    /// Get transfer count
    pub fn get_transfer_count(env: Env) -> u64 {
        env.storage().instance().get(&DataKey::TransferCount).unwrap_or(0)
    }

    /// Get token address
    pub fn get_token(env: Env) -> Address {
        env.storage().instance().get(&DataKey::TokenAddress).unwrap()
    }
}
```

### Example 4: Escrow Contract
```rust
#![no_std]
use soroban_sdk::{contract, contractimpl, contracttype, token, Address, Env, Symbol};

#[contracttype]
#[derive(Clone)]
pub enum DataKey {
    Escrow(u64),
    EscrowCount,
}

#[contracttype]
#[derive(Clone)]
pub struct EscrowData {
    pub depositor: Address,
    pub beneficiary: Address,
    pub token: Address,
    pub amount: i128,
    pub released: bool,
}

#[contract]
pub struct Escrow;

#[contractimpl]
impl Escrow {
    /// Create a new escrow
    pub fn create(
        env: Env,
        depositor: Address,
        beneficiary: Address,
        token_addr: Address,
        amount: i128,
    ) -> u64 {
        depositor.require_auth();
        
        // Transfer tokens to contract
        let token = token::Client::new(&env, &token_addr);
        token.transfer(&depositor, &env.current_contract_address(), &amount);
        
        // Get next escrow ID
        let mut count: u64 = env.storage().instance().get(&DataKey::EscrowCount).unwrap_or(0);
        count += 1;
        
        // Store escrow data
        let escrow = EscrowData {
            depositor: depositor.clone(),
            beneficiary: beneficiary.clone(),
            token: token_addr,
            amount,
            released: false,
        };
        
        env.storage().persistent().set(&DataKey::Escrow(count), &escrow);
        env.storage().instance().set(&DataKey::EscrowCount, &count);
        
        env.events().publish((Symbol::new(&env, "escrow_created"),), count);
        count
    }

    /// Release escrow to beneficiary
    pub fn release(env: Env, escrow_id: u64, releaser: Address) {
        releaser.require_auth();
        
        let key = DataKey::Escrow(escrow_id);
        let mut escrow: EscrowData = env.storage().persistent().get(&key).unwrap();
        
        if escrow.released {
            panic!("already released");
        }
        if releaser != escrow.depositor {
            panic!("only depositor can release");
        }
        
        // Transfer to beneficiary
        let token = token::Client::new(&env, &escrow.token);
        token.transfer(&env.current_contract_address(), &escrow.beneficiary, &escrow.amount);
        
        escrow.released = true;
        env.storage().persistent().set(&key, &escrow);
        
        env.events().publish((Symbol::new(&env, "escrow_released"),), escrow_id);
    }

    /// Get escrow details
    pub fn get_escrow(env: Env, escrow_id: u64) -> EscrowData {
        env.storage().persistent().get(&DataKey::Escrow(escrow_id)).unwrap()
    }
}
```

## Common Patterns

### Initialization Guard
```rust
pub fn initialize(env: Env, admin: Address) {
    if env.storage().instance().has(&DataKey::Admin) {
        panic!("already initialized");
    }
    env.storage().instance().set(&DataKey::Admin, &admin);
}
```

### Admin Check
```rust
fn require_admin(env: &Env, caller: &Address) {
    let admin: Address = env.storage().instance().get(&DataKey::Admin).unwrap();
    if caller != &admin {
        panic!("unauthorized: admin only");
    }
}
```

### Safe Math
```rust
// Use checked operations to prevent overflow
let new_balance = balance.checked_add(amount).expect("overflow");
let new_balance = balance.checked_sub(amount).expect("underflow");
```

## Type Reference

| Rust Type | Use For |
|-----------|---------|
| `i128` | Token amounts, balances |
| `u64` | Counters, IDs, timestamps |
| `u32` | Small counters, indices |
| `bool` | Flags |
| `Address` | Wallet/contract addresses |
| `String` | Text data |
| `Symbol` | Event names, map keys |
| `Vec<T>` | Lists |
| `Map<K, V>` | Key-value storage |
| `Bytes` | Raw byte data |
| `BytesN<N>` | Fixed-size bytes (e.g., hashes) |

## Stellar Network Info

- Testnet RPC: https://soroban-testnet.stellar.org
- Mainnet RPC: https://mainnet.sorobanrpc.com
- Mainnet Horizon: https://horizon.stellar.org
